pitching.name: Balance Point Pause Drill
pitching.description: At the top of the leg lift, hold the balance point for a 3-count before delivering. Builds proprioception and controlled load into the stride. Perform 15 reps in a bullpen session.
pitching.searchQuery: balance point pause drill baseball pitching mechanics

pitching.name: Hip-Shoulder Separation Band Drill
pitching.description: Place a resistance band around the torso at shoulder level. Fire the hips through delivery while the band resists the upper body, forcing the lower half to lead. Perform 3 sets of 10.
pitching.searchQuery: hip shoulder separation pitching resistance band drill baseball

pitching.name: Towel Drill
pitching.description: Hold a small towel in the throwing hand and deliver at full speed toward a target 10-12 feet away, snapping the towel at the target point. Forces a proper arm path and full extension through the release zone.
pitching.searchQuery: towel drill baseball pitching arm path extension

pitching.name: Knee Drill
pitching.description: Kneel on the throwing-side knee and throw full effort to a partner. Eliminates lower-half variables so the pitcher can isolate hip rotation sequencing and arm path without stride mechanics interfering.
pitching.searchQuery: kneeling delivery drill baseball pitching hip rotation arm path

pitching.name: Wall Arm Path Drill
pitching.mechanic: arm path, power zone, elbow height
pitching.description: Stand with the glove-side shoulder 6-8 inches from a wall. Execute the full arm circle — if the arm contacts the wall, it has broken outside the power zone. Demands an on-line, elbow-up arm path.
pitching.searchQuery: wall drill baseball pitching arm path power zone elbow

pitching.name: Stride Direction Tape Drill
pitching.description: Place a strip of tape from the rubber directly toward home plate. The stride foot should land on or just inside the tape line. Instant visual feedback on whether the pitcher strides open, closed, or on-line.
pitching.searchQuery: stride direction tape drill baseball pitching alignment

pitching.name: Glove Tuck and Pull Drill
pitching.description: In slow motion in front of a mirror, practice driving the glove toward the hip pocket as the throwing arm accelerates. Build the pattern in isolation until automatic, then integrate into full bullpen work.
pitching.searchQuery: glove tuck pull drill baseball pitching front side control

pitching.name: Cross-Body Follow-Through Drill
pitching.description: After each pitch, deliberately drive the throwing arm across the torso and finish with the hand outside the opposite hip. Trains proper deceleration mechanics and reduces shoulder and elbow stress.
pitching.searchQuery: cross body follow through deceleration drill baseball pitching

pitching.name: Long Toss Progression
pitching.description: Begin at 60 feet and extend distance to 90, 120, and 150+ feet on successive throws. Return trip compresses back to 60 feet. Builds posterior shoulder strength and reinforces extension through the release point.
pitching.searchQuery: long toss progression program baseball pitching arm strength

pitching.name: Drive Leg Push-Off Drill
pitching.mechanic: lower half drive, back leg extension
pitching.description: Place a foam roller or low hurdle behind the pivot foot. The drive leg must push up and over the barrier to complete the pitch. Teaches aggressive back-leg extension and proper weight transfer toward the plate.
pitching.searchQuery: drive leg push off drill baseball pitching lower half power

pitching.name: Plyo Ball Reverse Throw
pitching.description: Using a plyo ball and a padded wall, perform reverse throws — starting from the follow-through position and throwing backward into the wall. Strengthens posterior shoulder and trains the muscles responsible for deceleration.
pitching.searchQuery: plyo ball reverse throw deceleration baseball pitching arm health

pitching.name: Mirror Mechanics Drill
pitching.description: Perform the full delivery — leg lift, stride, arm action, and follow-through — in front of a full-length mirror. Watch for deviations in balance, arm path, and finish position. Repeat 15 reps per session.
pitching.searchQuery: mirror drill baseball pitching mechanics consistency self correction

pitching.name: Flat Ground Mechanics Work
pitching.description: Throw 30-50 pitches from flat ground at 70-80% intensity, focusing entirely on mechanical execution rather than velocity. Valuable for grooving movement patterns without mound fatigue.
pitching.searchQuery: flat ground work baseball pitching mechanics drill command

pitching.name: Spin Ball Hip Fire Drill
pitching.description: Hold a spin ball at chest height with both hands. Rotate the hips aggressively while keeping the ball and hands stationary as long as possible. Feel the torso stretch and release late. Isolates the hip-to-shoulder sequence.
pitching.searchQuery: hip fire rotational sequencing spin ball baseball pitching

pitching.name: Pause at K-Position Drill
pitching.mechanic: arm cocking, elbow at or above shoulder
pitching.description: At the K-position (arm cocked, elbow at shoulder height), pause for a 2-count and verify elbow height before delivering. Adding a light wrist weight increases proprioceptive feedback at that joint angle.
pitching.searchQuery: K position arm cocking elbow shoulder baseball pitching drill

pitching.name: Rocker Step Tempo Drill
pitching.description: Exaggerate a slow, deliberate rocker step and pause after the weight shifts back before lifting the knee. Rebuilds proper early-delivery timing and prevents rushing that collapses the balance point.
pitching.searchQuery: rocker step timing tempo drill baseball pitching footwork

pitching.name: High Sock Visual Feedback Drill
pitching.description: Pull socks up high and look down at the stride foot during slow-motion delivery work. The sock line gives clear visual reference for landing position relative to alignment. Use with tape drill for combined feedback.
pitching.searchQuery: high sock stride direction feedback baseball pitching visual

pitching.name: Short Distance Command Drill
pitching.description: Throw from 40-50 feet to a catcher, hitting a glove target with maximum precision at zero velocity emphasis. Builds release-point muscle memory and reinforces repeatable arm action through command reps.
pitching.searchQuery: short distance command drill baseball pitching release point consistency

batting.name: Tee Work Hip Rotation Focus
batting.description: Set a tee at the normal contact point. Take 20 swings focusing solely on the lower half: load the rear hip, plant the front heel, and fire the hips before the hands move. Hands stay passive until the hips have started turning.
batting.searchQuery: tee work hip rotation baseball hitting drill foundation

batting.name: Front Toss Timing Drill
batting.description: Coach soft-tosses from 15-20 feet slightly off-center. Focus on tracking the ball from release and driving it up the middle. Builds hand-eye coordination and bat-to-ball timing in a live-repetition setting.
batting.searchQuery: front toss timing drill baseball batting contact

batting.name: Hip Load and Trigger Drill
batting.description: From the stance, take an exaggerated hip load into the rear leg and hold for a 2-count before initiating the stride and swing. The extended pause builds proprioception of full hip loading before the trigger fires.
batting.searchQuery: hip load trigger drill baseball batting stance weight shift

batting.name: Heel Plant Hip Fire Drill
batting.mechanic: front heel timing, hip rotation trigger
batting.description: Take slow-motion tee swings focusing entirely on the front heel plant sequence. The heel must drive into the ground before the hands move. No velocity emphasis — this is feel work for the trigger mechanism.
batting.searchQuery: front heel plant hip fire baseball hitting trigger drill

batting.name: Bottom-Hand Extension Drill
batting.description: Remove the top hand from the bat after contact and extend through the ball using only the bottom hand, holding the finish for a 2-count. Develops lead-arm extension and prevents rolling over at contact.
batting.searchQuery: one hand bottom hand extension drill baseball hitting lead arm

batting.name: Top-Hand Barrel Control Drill
batting.mechanic: top hand, barrel path, bat control
batting.description: Swing using only the top hand on the bat. Keep the barrel above the hands through the swing plane and drive it to the contact zone. Builds top-hand strength and barrel awareness for staying on plane.
batting.searchQuery: one hand top hand barrel control drill baseball hitting

batting.name: Inside-Out Tee Drill
batting.mechanic: hands inside the ball, opposite-field contact
batting.description: Place the tee on the inner third of the plate. Keep the hands tight to the body and drive the ball to the opposite field. Eliminates casting the barrel and builds the hands-inside mechanical pattern.
batting.searchQuery: inside out tee drill baseball hitting hands inside the ball

batting.name: High-Low Tee Drill
batting.description: Set two tees — one at belt height and one at knee height. Alternate swings between levels while maintaining consistent hip rotation and barrel path at each height. Develops adaptable swing plane.
batting.searchQuery: high low tee drill swing plane baseball hitting pitch level

batting.name: Rear Hip Hinge Load Drill
batting.description: Stand with the rear hip touching a wall. As the load begins, the hip moves away from the wall (hinging back and down). If the hip stays against the wall, the hitter is swaying rather than hinging. Wall provides instant feedback.
batting.searchQuery: rear hip hinge load wall drill baseball hitting weight shift

batting.name: Staying Back Off-Speed Drill
batting.description: A coach mixes fastballs and off-speed from a machine or front toss. Focus on keeping weight loaded until the ball's trajectory is confirmed. Off-speed pitches are driven to the opposite field.
batting.searchQuery: staying back off speed drill baseball hitting timing weight shift

batting.name: Extension and Finish Drill
batting.description: After each swing, hold the finish position for a 3-count. Arms should be fully extended, front elbow up, and weight balanced on the front side. Ingrains a complete, powerful follow-through.
batting.searchQuery: extension follow through finish drill baseball hitting

batting.name: Heavy Bag Hip Rotation Drill
batting.description: Rotate through the swing and drive both hands into a heavy bag positioned at the contact point. The bag's resistance demands maximum hip rotation and prevents arm-dominant swings from generating power.
batting.searchQuery: heavy bag hip rotation power drill baseball hitting core

batting.name: Mirror Stance and Load Check
batting.description: Stand in front of a mirror and perform the full load-and-stride sequence without swinging. Check for symmetry in setup, balance during load, and consistent stride direction. Correct any pre-swing inconsistencies visually.
batting.searchQuery: mirror stance load check drill baseball hitting mechanics

batting.name: Rapid-Fire Front Toss
batting.description: A coach tosses balls in quick succession with minimal rest. The hitter resets stance rapidly and takes full swings on each toss. Trains fast-twitch response, bat speed under fatigue, and mental presence.
batting.searchQuery: rapid fire front toss bat speed drill baseball hitting reaction

batting.name: Overload and Underload Bat Speed
batting.description: Alternate sets of swings with a heavier-than-game bat (overload) and a lighter-than-game bat (underload). The neurological contrast between heavy and light develops explosive bat speed through contrast training.
batting.searchQuery: overload underload bat speed contrast training baseball hitting

batting.name: Hip-Shoulder Separation Band Drill
batting.description: Wrap a resistance band around the shoulders. During each swing, fire the hips fully while the band slows the shoulder turn. Forces awareness of the separation between lower and upper half and builds torque.
batting.searchQuery: hip shoulder separation band drill baseball hitting rotational lag

batting.name: No-Stride Hip Isolation Drill
batting.description: Start with feet already planted in the launch position — no stride taken. Swing focusing entirely on hip rotation and keeping hands inside the ball. Strips stride variables so the hitter can isolate and feel pure hip fire.
batting.searchQuery: no stride hip isolation drill baseball hitting lower half rotation

batting.name: Contact Point Fence Drill
batting.description: Stand with a fence or wall about one bat-length behind the hitter. Take full swings — if the barrel hits the fence on the backswing, the arc is too wide. Forces a direct, compact path to the ball.
batting.searchQuery: fence drill contact point prevent casting baseball hitting compact

batting.name: Rotational Plyo Ball Wall Work
batting.description: Stand sideways to a solid wall or rebounder. Rotate aggressively and throw a plyo ball into the wall, catching the rebound. Builds rotational core power that transfers directly to bat speed and hip rotation.
batting.searchQuery: rotational plyo ball wall work baseball hitting core power

batting.name: Stride Direction and Length Drill
batting.description: Place tape from the back foot directly toward the pitcher. The stride foot should land 6-8 inches inside the tape line. Consistent stride direction creates consistent contact zones and removes lateral variance.
batting.searchQuery: stride direction length drill baseball hitting mechanics consistency

classicPitchers.name: Greg Maddux
classicPitchers.note: textbook hip-to-shoulder separation, elbow always in front, glove tucked — 23 seasons minimal arm issues

classicPitchers.name: Clayton Kershaw
classicPitchers.note: elite balance point, upper-90s hip-shoulder separation, arm path on-line, deceleration across body

classicPitchers.name: Justin Verlander
classicPitchers.note: controlled rocker step, powerful hip rotation before shoulder, late loose arm swing

classicPitchers.name: Nolan Ryan
classicPitchers.note: 27 seasons — maximum hip-shoulder separation, full lower-half drive, tremendous follow-through

classicPitchers.name: Pedro Martinez
classicPitchers.note: explosive hip rotation, compact arm path, glove tight to chest, elite extension

classicPitchers.name: Tom Seaver
classicPitchers.note: drop-and-drive lower half, powerful back leg push, spine angle maintained throughout

classicPitchers.name: Sandy Koufax
classicPitchers.note: pure arm path every pitch, balanced upright posture, full extension, clean follow-through

classicPitchers.name: Max Scherzer
classicPitchers.note: arm in power zone every pitch, glove side disciplined, follow-through across body

classicPitchers.name: Logan Webb
classicPitchers.note: clean repeatable mechanics praised by scouts, minimal wasted movement, low arm stress per outing

classicPitchers.name: Mariano Rivera
classicPitchers.note: most repeatable delivery in history — same arm path every single pitch, textbook deceleration

currentPitchers.name: Tarik Skubal

currentPitchers.name: Gerrit Cole

currentPitchers.name: Zack Wheeler

currentPitchers.name: Spencer Strider

currentPitchers.name: Blake Snell
currentPitchers.note: dramatic hip-shoulder separation, high leg kick with controlled balance, late arm entry

currentPitchers.name: Framber Valdez
currentPitchers.note: elite sinker mechanics built on consistent arm path and strong lower half drive

currentPitchers.name: Shane Bieber
currentPitchers.note: pinpoint command mechanics, repeatable release point, efficient hip-to-shoulder sequence

currentPitchers.name: Chris Sale
currentPitchers.note: elite extension through the release zone, unique arm slot with clean and safe deceleration pattern

classicBatters.name: Ted Williams
classicBatters.note: most studied swing in history, perfect hip-to-shoulder sequence, bat path stays in the zone longest of any hitter ever analyzed

classicBatters.name: Babe Ruth
classicBatters.note: legendary hip rotation and weight transfer, generated elite power from explosive lower-half drive

classicBatters.name: Hank Aaron
classicBatters.note: exceptional quick wrists and compact path to contact, bat speed generated late through the zone

classicBatters.name: Willie Mays
classicBatters.note: balanced athletic load and explosive hip turn, exceptional timing with consistent barrel path

classicBatters.name: Mickey Mantle
classicBatters.note: elite switch-hitter mechanics, explosive lower half generated power from both sides equally

classicBatters.name: Stan Musial
classicBatters.note: unusual coiled stance but perfectly repeatable, proved mechanics only need to be consistent not textbook

classicBatters.name: Barry Bonds
classicBatters.note: most biomechanically precise swing ever studied, elite hip rotation with exceptional barrel control through the zone

classicBatters.name: Tony Gwynn
classicBatters.note: greatest contact mechanics in modern baseball, short path to ball with exceptional hands-inside discipline

classicBatters.name: Ken Griffey Jr.
classicBatters.note: widely considered the most aesthetically perfect swing, effortless hip rotation with elite extension and follow-through

classicBatters.name: Lou Gehrig
classicBatters.note: powerful consistent upper-body mechanics, exceptional hands through the zone with elite weight transfer

currentBatters.name: Shohei Ohtani
currentBatters.note: elite hip rotation and bat speed from exceptional lower half, generates elite power while maintaining barrel accuracy

currentBatters.name: Mike Trout
currentBatters.note: gold standard modern hitting mechanics, elite barrel control with consistent hip-to-shoulder separation

currentBatters.name: Freddie Freeman
currentBatters.note: textbook weight transfer and consistent barrel path, exceptional extension through the zone

currentBatters.name: Mookie Betts
currentBatters.note: compact controlled swing with elite bat-to-ball skills, exceptional hip turn from minimal load

currentBatters.name: Juan Soto
currentBatters.note: exceptional hip load and patience-driven contact approach, elite ability to stay back on off-speed pitches

currentBatters.name: Yordan Alvarez
currentBatters.note: massive power from elite hip-to-shoulder separation, exceptional rear leg drive generating elite exit velocity

currentBatters.name: Ronald Acuna Jr.
currentBatters.note: explosive athleticism with quick hands through the zone, exceptional first-move quickness with elite bat speed

currentBatters.name: Corey Seager
currentBatters.note: smooth lefty mechanics with consistent extension, elite hip turn with exceptional barrel path to all fields

currentBatters.name: Bobby Witt Jr.
currentBatters.note: elite bat speed with athletic lower half, emerging as one of the most mechanically explosive young hitters
